forbiddenClaims: that the authenticated patient-record route was inspected
silently cloning the DOM or the screenshot without resolving the disagreement
a new data-collection surface: symptom-checker, triage widget, PHI form, or tracker not present on the inspected routes
adapted clinical content presented as authoritative medical guidance without a 'not clinical advice' framing
regression of accessible-language, reading-level, contrast, or non-English-language access
reproduction of the source portal's proprietary copy, masthead, exact palette, illustrations, or distinctive iconography
affiliation with any real healthcare provider, regulator, clinical body, or EHR vendor

rubricAnchors.score1: The adaptation silently clones one signal of the disagreement, introduces a new data-collection surface, or presents clinical content as authoritative.
rubricAnchors.score3: The disagreement is acknowledged but the resolution is fuzzy, or the not-clinical-advice framing is present only in a footer.
rubricAnchors.score5: The DOM/screenshot disagreement is resolved with a recorded decision, no new data-collection surface is introduced, and every clinical block carries an anchored not-clinical-advice framing.

rubricAnchors.score1: Service-information and record-boundary share no common disagreement-resolution or compliance model.
rubricAnchors.score3: Some shared tokens but the not-clinical-advice framing or boundary marking drifts between the two screens.
rubricAnchors.score5: A single direction system unifies service-information and record-boundary, including a consistent disagreement-resolution treatment and a consistent authenticated-route-boundary marking.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer the dom-screenshot-disagreement-resolved, not-clinical-advice-visible, authenticated-route-blocked, or no-new-data-collection-surface states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved dom-screenshot-disagreement-resolved or authenticated-route-blocked states.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build service-information and record-boundary directly, including all declared states, the explicit disagreement-resolution decision recorded in code, and the no-new-data-collection-surface guard made explicit.

rubricAnchors.score1: A verbatim copy of the source portal's copy, masthead, palette, illustrations, or distinctive iconography.
rubricAnchors.score3: Generic principles adapted but a proprietary identifier (masthead, illustration, icon) slips through.
rubricAnchors.score5: Only generic structural principles are retained or adapted; proprietary identifiers and clinical content presented as authoritative are explicitly rejected with recorded decisions.

rubricAnchors.score1: Accessible-language, reading-level, contrast, or non-English-language access regresses; no service-info-load-error or boundary-load-error handling.
rubricAnchors.score3: Accessibility is mostly preserved but reading-level or non-English-language access drifts on one route.
rubricAnchors.score5: Accessible-language, reading-level, contrast, and non-English-language access are preserved across both screens, with robust service-info-load-error, authenticated-route-blocked, and boundary-load-error states.

rubricAnchors.score1: Claims rely on the authenticated patient-record route, an invented clinical finding, or a fabricated service not in the snapshot.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to inspected routes but one claim overreaches into the authenticated record or invents a service.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to an inspected route, the authenticated patient-record route is explicitly excluded, and the DOM/screenshot disagreement resolution is recorded with its evidence basis.

adjudicationNotes: The DOM/screenshot disagreement is load-bearing: silently cloning either signal fails product-appropriateness and evidence-discipline.
No new data-collection surface is load-bearing: introducing a symptom-checker, triage widget, or PHI form fails product-appropriateness and accessibility-and-failure-states regardless of intent.
The authenticated patient-record route was NOT inspected; any claim relying on it fails evidence-discipline.
Clinical content must carry an anchored not-clinical-advice framing; burying it in a footer fails accessibility-and-failure-states.
Accessible-language preservation is non-negotiable for a regulated health surface; regressions fail accessibility-and-failure-states.
